Routing

This example shows two ways to drive component state from the browser hash: a minimal panel switcher using #panel=..., and a path-style router using #route=/....

Manual Hash Panels

Use a selected hash cell directly when all you need is on/off panel state.

#panel=home,route=/
Current panel: home

Home

Bind a component to state.hash.select(["panel"]) and derive booleans from that value.

Router-Based Panels

Use router() when the hash value should behave like a small path router with matching and captures.

#panel=home,route=/
Current route: /

Home Route

The routed version maps a path-like hash value such as #route=/settings to components.

  • Simple routes: /, /settings
  • Captured routes: /profile/{tab}
